hypothesis: If neural network Bayesian Algorithm Execution (NN-BAX) is applied to nudged elastic band calculations by iteratively fine-tuning a pre-trained equivariant neural network foundation model (EquiformerV2) and strategically acquiring training points along predicted minimum energy pathways, then it will identify accurate transition pathways between local minima with significantly fewer expensive simulator evaluations than classical NEB, because the surrogate model learns to approximate the potential energy surface in the relevant regions of configuration space through targeted, information-efficient sampling guided by the BAX acquisition strategy.
